stories_covered: Grock acquired by NVIDIA for $20B in cash before Christmas
Navan trading at 4X ARR, recently went public
Databricks, Revolut, Stripe staying private despite massive valuations
Cerebrus planning IPO as Grock competitor
Invisible unemployment: Stanford grads unable to find jobs
Entry-level sales and support roles disappearing to AI agents
harry_takes: Inference workloads, not just training, are the real growth driver in AI infrastructure as knowledge workers run models 24/7
Nvidia acquiring Grock was strategic necessity at $20B to eliminate a potential margin-pressure competitor, not a revenue play
The Grock deal sets a psychological comp that normalizes multi-billion dollar acquisition prices for other tech giants
When private markets offer better risk-adjusted returns than public markets, founders rationally stay private and even self-fund via dividends
Invisible unemployment is emerging: senior executives and entry-level workers face dislocation while top-tier AI talent earns $1-4M
harry_boldest_quote: The public markets have a problem.
predictions: Jason Lemkin: Entry-level sales jobs (SDRs, support) will vanish entirely in 2026, with only top-tier talent finding roles at Anthropic and OpenAI
Jason Lemkin: Mid-career workers with pre-2021 skillsets will quietly exit the workforce rather than reskill
Rory O'Driscoll: Databricks will eventually go public to gain M&A firepower, enabling $10-50B acquisition strategy not available when private
Rory O'Driscoll: Oracle/Broadcom margin pressures reflect temporary jitters; semiconductor capex cycle has 2+ years of strong legs remaining
disagreements: Lemkin and O'Driscoll disagree on severity of youth unemployment. Lemkin warns of broader dislocation and invisible unemployment across all experience levels. O'Driscoll is less concerned but concedes 22-24 year olds with Stanford degrees will face visible stress, making populist sentiment likely.
harry_role: Harry poses framing questions, clarifies valuations and comparisons, and pushes panelists toward second-order implications. He challenges the disconnect between startup founder ages (young, grinders) and hiring discrimination, and emphasizes systemic labor-market shifts over temporary cycles.
notable: Striking breakdown of how Grock, founded 2016-17 by ex-Google TPU engineers, took 8 years to reach $50M revenue but captured $20B valuation via strategic acquisition—illustrating the venture binary between multi-decade slog and sudden windfall, and the rarity of semiconductor exits despite AI momentum.
